ddls:
CREATE TABLE "exceptions" (
  "exception_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each exception */,
  "exception_type" INTEGER /* ID of the exception type */,
  "exception_code" INTEGER /* ID of the exception code */,
  "real_mode" INTEGER /* Indicates if the exception occurred in real mode */,
  "virtual_mode" INTEGER /* Indicates if the exception occurred in virtual mode */,
  "protected_mode" INTEGER /* Indicates if the exception occurred in protected mode */,
  "cause_of_exception" INTEGER /* ID of the cause of the exception */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the exception occurred */,
  "system_id" INTEGER /* ID of the system where the exception occurred */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the exception (e.g., low, medium, high, critical) */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the exception has been resolved */,
  "resolution_date" TEXT /* Date and time the exception was resolved */,
  "resolution_details" TEXT /* Detailed description of how the exception was resolved */,
  "alert_generated" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if an alert was generated for this exception */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* ID of the report linked to this exception */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("exception_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_exceptions_report_id FOREIGN KEY ("report_id") REFERENCES reports ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_exceptions_system_id FOREIGN KEY ("system_id") REFERENCES systems ("system_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_exceptions_cause_of_exception FOREIGN KEY ("cause_of_exception") REFERENCES causes_of_exception ("cause_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_exceptions_exception_type FOREIGN KEY ("exception_type") REFERENCES exception_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_exceptions_exception_code FOREIGN KEY ("exception_code") REFERENCES exception_codes ("code_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "systems" (
  "system_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each system */,
  "system_name" TEXT /* Name of the system */,
  "system_type" TEXT /* Type of the system (e.g., server, embedded device) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the system */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the system (e.g., online, offline) */,
  "last_checked" TEXT /* Date and time the system was last checked */,
  "os_version" TEXT /* Version of the operating system running on the system */,
  "hardware_version" TEXT /* Version of the hardware */,
  "last_reboot" TEXT /* Date and time of the last system reboot */,
  "uptime" INTEGER /* Current uptime of the system in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("system_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "exception_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each exception type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the exception type (e.g., Stack, General protection) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the exception type */,
  "severity_level" INTEGER /* Numeric value representing the severity level of the exception type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "exception_codes" (
  "code_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each exception code */,
  "code_name" TEXT /* Code of the exception (e.g., #SS, #GP) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the exception code */,
  "severity_level" INTEGER /* Numeric value representing the severity level of the exception code */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("code_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "causes_of_exception" (
  "cause_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cause */,
  "cause_description" TEXT /* Detailed cause of the exception */,
  "recommended_action" TEXT /* Suggested action to resolve the exception */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cause_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "modes" (
  "mode_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mode */,
  "mode_name" TEXT /* Name of the mode (e.g., Real, Virtual, Protected) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the mode */,
  "default_mode" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if this mode is the default mode for the system */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mode_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "exception_modes" (
  "exception_mode_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each exception mode mapping */,
  "exception_id" INTEGER /* ID of the exception */,
  "mode_id" INTEGER /* ID of the mode */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("exception_mode_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_exception_modes_mode_id FOREIGN KEY ("mode_id") REFERENCES modes ("mode_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_exception_modes_exception_id FOREIGN KEY ("exception_id") REFERENCES exceptions ("exception_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, developer, analyst) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  "last_activity" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last activity */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "exception_id" INTEGER /* ID of the exception being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the exception */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date and time of access */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, modify) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used to access the system */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_exception_id FOREIGN KEY ("exception_id") REFERENCES exceptions ("exception_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "exception_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each history entry */,
  "exception_id" INTEGER /* ID of the exception */,
  "change_type" TEXT /* Type of change (e.g., create, update, delete) */,
  "change_description" TEXT /* Description of the change */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the change */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the change */,
  "previous_value" TEXT /* Previous value of the field that was changed */,
  "current_value" TEXT /* Current value of the field that was changed */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Brief reason for the change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_exception_history_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_exception_history_exception_id FOREIGN KEY ("exception_id") REFERENCES exceptions ("exception_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of the report (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly) */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date and time the report was generated */,
  "report_file_path" TEXT /* File path to the report */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the report (e.g., generated, reviewed, approved) */,
  "reviewed_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who reviewed the report */,
  "review_date" TEXT /* Date and time the report was reviewed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_reviewed_by FOREIGN KEY ("reviewed_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "exception_id" INTEGER /* ID of the exception */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of the alert (e.g., critical, warning) */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message of the alert */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date and time the alert was generated */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who received the alert */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the alert (e.g., active, resolved, ignored) */,
  "resolved_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who resolved the alert */,
  "resolved_date" TEXT /* Date and time the alert was resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_exception_id FOREIGN KEY ("exception_id") REFERENCES exceptions ("exception_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_resolved_by FOREIGN KEY ("resolved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
